texto: Hope? Hope is not the absence of tragedy, my friend. It is the conviction that tragedy can be endured. Hope is the spark in you that is not subdued in the face of the vast and callous indifference of the universe. Hope is that which is not shattered by hardship. Hope is the urge to fight what is wrong even when you know it will destroy you. Hope is the decision to love and need someone knowing that they will one day die. For me to promise that there are no obstacles would be the cruelest lie I could possibly tell. That lie is not hope. Hope is the will which needs no lies.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Travis Beacham que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Hope? Hope is not the absence of tragedy, my friend. It is the conviction that tragedy can be end…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, esperanca, inspiracional, com foco em inspiracao e termino.

texto: I've never believed in the end of times. We are mankind. Our footprints are on the moon. When the last trumpet sounds and the beast rises from the pit---we will kill it.
